passage.title: Shade Garden Test
passage.text: Our garden club tested two places for young lettuce plants last month. Mina used the hot sunny bed, but Joon used the light shade bed. After one week, Joon's plants stayed greener because the shade kept the soil from drying so fast. The club measured leaf size and wrote each change in a notebook. When Mina saw the difference, she changed to the light shade bed. Two days later, her plants also looked fresher in the afternoon. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that the right amount of shade can help young plants.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning which place helps young plants stay healthy
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: Where did Joon put his young plants?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does shade mean in the passage?
choices: A place with less direct sunlight
A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Mina move her plants to the light shade bed?
choices: She thought the plants needed less hot sun
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
